summary: A nicely mixed matrix captures a 1995 show where Garcia’s frail health produces both heroic moments and occasional awkwardness.
review: The matrix recording is praised as “really nice,” delivering clear, balanced sound that lets listeners hear the nuances of this late‑era performance. While Jerry Garcia’s health issues sometimes make the playing feel uneasy, there are also flashes of heroism that keep the show compelling despite the lack of specific standout songs.

ai_rating.rationale: Only one detailed review; rating reflects a solid performance with good matrix quality but acknowledges variable playing due to Garcia’s health.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewer describes the matrix as "really nice" and thanks the engineer, indicating clear, well‑balanced sound with no noted issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Performance varies because of Garcia’s health—moments of heroic playing are interspersed with occasional awkwardness.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia was in poor health at this time; his playing swings between awkward and heroic, making the show especially interesting.
